top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 81, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
87ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Eroica Germania's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
At Eroica Germania, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Eroica Germania. Train it until it feels boring.
Eroica Germania's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 81 miles.
7,047ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 81 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
7,047ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 81 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 81 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,047ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: August in Eltville am Rhein, Hesse, Germany can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Eroica Germania. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Eroica Germania’s 81 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 81 miles of mixed terrain in Eltville am Rhein, Hesse, Germany.
